title: Cnemidaria subglabra
taxonomy: Cnemidaria subglabra is a species of tree fern. It belongs to the genus Cnemidaria, which is a group of ferns found in tropical and subtropical regions. Cnemidaria subglabra is classified within the family Cyatheaceae, commonly known as the tree fern family. This family is a part of the division Pteridophyta, which includes all types of ferns and fern allies. The classification of Cnemidaria subglabra reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Cnemidaria subglabra, also known as the "Resurrection Fern," is a type of epiphytic fern native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Cnemidaria subglabra include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often consumed to promote relaxation, reduce inflammation, and support immune function.
